audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Israel not rejected, remnant, jealousy, olive tree warning, mystery, all Israel, mercy on all, doxology. Strong anti-replacement safeguards required.

verses.restored: I say then, did the Divine reject his people? May it not be. For I also am an Israelite, from seed of Abraham, tribe of Benjamin.
The Divine did not reject his people whom he foreknew. Or do you not know what the Scripture says in Elijah, how he appeals to the Divine against Israel?
Lord, they killed your prophets; they tore down your altars; I alone was left, and they seek my life.
But what does the divine answer say to him? I left for myself seven thousand men who did not bend knee to Baal.
So then also in the present time there has come to be a remnant according to choosing of grace.
But if by grace, no longer from works; otherwise grace no longer becomes grace.
What then? What Israel seeks, this it did not obtain, but the chosen obtained; the rest were hardened,
just as it is written: The Divine gave them a spirit of deep sleep, eyes not to see and ears not to hear, until this day.
And David says: Let their table become a snare and a trap and a stumbling block and a repayment to them;
let their eyes be darkened not to see, and bend their back through all.
I say then, did they stumble so that they might fall? May it not be. But by their trespass salvation/healing has come to the nations, to provoke them to jealousy.
Now if their trespass is riches of world, and their loss riches of nations, how much more their fullness?
But I speak to you, the nations. Inasmuch then as I am apostle of nations, I glorify my service,
if somehow I might provoke my flesh to jealousy and save/heal some of them.
For if their rejection is reconciliation of world, what will their acceptance be if not life from the dead?
If the firstfruit is holy, also the lump; and if the root is holy, also the branches.
But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, being wild olive, were grafted in among them and became sharer of the rich root of the olive tree,
do not boast over the branches. But if you boast, remember: you do not support the root, but the root supports you.
You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in.
Beautifully/well; by untrust they were broken off, but you stand by trust. Do not be high-minded, but fear/revere.
For if the Divine did not spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you.
See therefore kindness and severity of the Divine: upon those who fell, severity; but upon you, kindness of the Divine, if you remain in kindness; otherwise you also will be cut off.
And those also, if they do not remain in untrust, will be grafted in; for the Divine is powerful to graft them in again.
For if you were cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree and against nature were grafted into cultivated olive, how much more will these natural ones be grafted into their own olive tree?
For I do not want you to be unaware, siblings, of this mystery, so that you may not be wise in yourselves: a hardening in part has happened to Israel until the fullness of the nations comes in,
and so all Israel will be saved/healed, just as it is written: The Deliverer will come from Zion; he will turn away ungodliness from Jacob.
And this is the covenant from me to them, when I take away their sins/misalignments.
According to the good news, they are opposed for your sake; but according to choosing, beloved because of the fathers.
For the gifts and the calling of the Divine are irrevocable.
For just as you once disobeyed the Divine, but now were shown mercy by their disobedience,
so also these now disobeyed by the mercy shown to you, so that they also now may be shown mercy.
For the Divine enclosed all into disobedience, so that he might show mercy to all.
O depth of riches and wisdom and knowledge of the Divine. How unsearchable his judgments and untraceable his ways.
For who knew the mind of the Lord? Or who became his counselor?
Or who first gave to him, and it will be repaid to him?
Because from him and through him and into him are all things. To him be glory into the ages. Amen.

safety_note: Romans 11 explicitly rejects the idea that the Divine rejected Israel. It warns nations-believers not to boast over Israel and grounds the argument in irrevocable gifts/calling and mercy on all. This chapter is a major anti-replacement anchor.
